title: Silky Floral Kenyan Tuna Stew
description: A fragrant, silky stew that blends Kenyan flavors with a touch of floral sweetness from basil and cherry tomatoes, featuring tender tuna simmered to perfection.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium‑high heat. Add diced onion and sauté until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add minced garlic, ginger, smoked paprika, and ground coriander; cook, stirring, until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Stir in cherry tomatoes and cook until they begin to soften, about 3–4 minutes.
Pour in vegetable broth and bring to a gentle simmer; let the mixture reduce slightly, about 5 minutes.
Gently fold in the coconut milk, stirring to combine; maintain a low simmer.
Carefully add the cubed tuna pieces, ensuring they are submerged; cover and simmer for 6–8 minutes, or until the tuna reaches 145°F internally.
Season the stew with salt and pepper to taste.
Remove from heat and stir in the torn basil leaves for a fresh floral note.
Serve hot over steamed rice or with crusty gluten‑free bread.
